title: ZREVRANGEBYLEX
description: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.
syntax: ZREVRANGEBYLEX key max min [LIMIT offset count]
complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements being returned. If M is constant (e.g. always asking for the first 10 elements with LIMIT), you can consider it O(log(N)).

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
When all the elements in a sorted set are inserted with the same score, in order to force lexicographical ordering, this command returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a value between `max` and `min`. Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYLEX` is similar to [`ZRANGEBYLEX`](). ## Examples ZADD myzset 0 a 0 b 0 c 0 d 0 e 0 f 0 g ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset [c - ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset (c - ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset (g [aaa ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | Deprecated as of Redis v6.2.0. | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified score range. **RESP3:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): List of the elements in the specified score range.
intro:
When all the elements in a sorted set are inserted with the same score, in order to force lexicographical ordering, this command returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a value between `max` and `min`.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYLEX` is similar to [`ZRANGEBYLEX`]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
When all the elements in a sorted set are inserted with the same score, in order to force lexicographical ordering, this command returns all the elements in the sorted set at `key` with a value between `max` and `min`.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGEBYLEX` is similar to [`ZRANGEBYLEX`]().

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
ZADD myzset 0 a 0 b 0 c 0 d 0 e 0 f 0 g
ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset [c -
ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset (c -
ZREVRANGEBYLEX myzset (g [aaa

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified score range.

**RESP3:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): List of the elements in the specified score range.
